title: Privacy Policy
text: Privacy Policy Effective Date: December 16, 2024 Cavallo International Head Office 1714 Lockyer Rd, Roberts Creek, British Columbia, CANADA V0N 2W1 Cavallo is committed to protecting your privacy. This Privacy Policy outlines how we collect, use, disclose, and protect your personal information in compliance with international privacy laws, including but not limited to the General Data Protection Regulation (GDPR) , California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) , and other applicable regulations. 1. Email Privacy Policy Use and Protection of Your Information Cavallo has implemented a comprehensive policy to ensure the protection of your personal information, including your email address, from misuse. We take your privacy seriously and handle your information as outlined below: Sharing of Personal Data Cavallo does not sell, rent, or disclose your personal data or email address to third parties without your explicit consent, except as required by law (e.g., court orders) or as agreed upon in a separate agreement. Third-Party Use Subscriber and customer data will never be sold, rented, or loaned to third parties for any purpose. Cavallo may utilize secure, third-party software to distribute newsletters, store order information, or manage subscription services. title: How to Measure
h1: How To Measure for Cavallo Hoof Boots
text: How To Measure for Cavallo Hoof Boots Measuring video Cavallo Boots are SIMPLE to MEASURE. Learn How to Measure and Fit your Cavallos with this Easy Video! How to Test the Fit of Your Cavallo Hoof Boots Cavallo Hoof Boots are Easy to Fit! Simply insert the hoof into the boot. Secure Velcro® closures, and then secure outside closure(s) on the boot. Important Tips on Fitting You should not have to force the boot on the horse’s hoof in any way. If you have to force the boot on, it is too small. If the boot is rotating independently from the horse’s hoof, it is too big. A wiggle is okay because the hoof expands in weight bearing and even further in movement. Only new, UNUSED boots can be exchanged. To keep the boot clean, we suggest covering the hoof with thin plastic and checking the fit on a non-scuff (not gravel), clean, dry surface. How Should Your Horse's Hoof Fit Into a Cavallo Boot? Here you can see how the hoof sole fits into the length of the boot, and the heel bulbs fit into the padded heel area at the back of the boot. Boot Break-In Guide Breaking In your New Boots Horse owners should allow their horses to ‘break in’ their boots gradually, starting with 15 – 20 minutes per day and increasing from there, while monitoring for comfort. Using Cavallos in water or applying a leather conditioner to “work” the leather at the heel bulb will hasten the softening progress. Cavallo

title: Get Boot Smart
text: Get Boot Smart Ask an Expert Real horse owners standing by to help you! Want to chat with a Cavallo Hoof Boots expert? We’re here for you! Whether you need help measuring, deciding which Cavallo boot style suits your horse, want to learn about using Cavallos for hoof rehabilitation or have questions about barefoot hoof care, our experienced team is ready to assist. We also offer a free expert sizing service to take the worry out of getting the right fit, whether your horse is barefoot or shod. Let us make your hoof boot journey easy, informed, and worry-free! Reach out now! Benefits of Barefoot Hoof Care Natural and Healthier Hoof Care Barefoot hoof care offers a more natural and healthier approach for your horse. Removing metal shoes allows hooves to function properly, promoting better blood circulation and shock absorption. A barefoot regimen requires regular trims, adequate exercise, and proper hoof hydration. This approach supports the hoof’s natural ability to expand and absorb impact, leading to overall improved hoof health. Embrace modern technology with Cavallo Hoof Boots as an alternative to traditional shoeing. title: FAQ
text: Frequently Asked Questions Can we help you further? Looking for an answer to your booting questions? Ask our team here: Ask an Expert How can I determine if my horse's saddle fits properly? Put your saddle on with no padding underneath, and do not cinch up. Try to run your hand in between your horse and the saddle from the front of the saddle all the way along the muscular structure under the bars of the saddle. Your hand should be in a palm-up position with the back of your hand against your horse and your palm feeling along the bottom of your saddle. In this way, your palm will feel what your horse feels. Notice if your hand slides in easily and whether there are any discrepancies in the feel. Does the saddle fit tightly in some areas and looser in others? Is it tighter on one side than the other? Notice how it feels. Can you feel the bottom of screws, rivets or conches? Is it uniform and consistent or rough and uneven? Your job is to determine what the underside of the saddle feels like on your horse’s back and then be better informed to select the appropriate Cavallo Total Comfort System Saddle Pad to correct the discrepancies and irregularities. Can Cavallo pads be used with a treeless saddle? All TCS pads are excellent with treeless saddles because they create structure and ensure that weight is minimized on the skeletal structure.
